channel.title: Dong Xing's Blog
channel.description: Last 10 notes on Dong Xing's Blog

item.title: Tarjan's Bridge-Finding Algorithm
item.description: Tarjan’s Bridge-Finding Algorithm is an algorithm that finds the bridges in an undirected graph G=(V,E) in \mathcal{O}(|V|+|E|) time and \mathcal{O}(|V|+|E|) space.

item.title: Tarjan's Cut-Vertex-Finding Algorithm
item.description: Tarjan’s Cut-Vertex-Finding Algorithm is an algorithm that finds the cut vertices in an undirected graph G=(V,E) in \mathcal{O}(|V|+|E|) time and \mathcal{O}(|V|+|E|) space.

item.title: Tarjan's Strongly Connected Components Algorithm
item.description: Tarjan’s Strongly Connected Components Algorithm is an algorithm that finds the strongly connected components of a directed graph G=(V,E) in \mathcal{O}(|V|+|E|) and \mathcal{O}(|V|+|E|) space.

item.title: Kosaraju's Algorithm
item.description: Kosaraju’s Algorithm is an algorithm that finds the strongly connected components of a directed graph G=(V,E) in \mathcal{O}(|V|+|E|) and \mathcal{O}(|V|+|E|) space.

item.title: Euler's Sieve
item.description: Euler’s Sieve is an algorithm that finds the primes in 2,3,\dots,n in \mathcal{O}(n) time and \mathcal{O}(n) space.

item.title: Sieve of Eratosthenes
item.description: The Sieve of Eratosthenes is an algorithm that finds the primes in 2,3,\dots,n in \mathcal{O}(n\log\log n) time and \mathcal{O}(n) space.

item.title: Aho-Corasick Automaton
item.description: The Aho-Corasick Automaton is a data structure that maintains a set S of strings in \Sigma^* by maintaining a deterministic finite automaton that accepts and only accepts strings containing a string from S as a suffix.

item.title: Segment Tree
item.description: The Segment Tree is a data structure that maintains an array of n=2^k numbers a_0,a_1,\dots,a_{n-1} by maintaining s(0,2^k),s(0,2^{k-1}),s(2^{k-1},2^k),\dots,s(0,1),s(1,2),\dots,s(2^k-1,2^k), where s(l,r)=\sum_{i=l}^{r-1}a_i which costs a space of \mathcal{O}(n).

item.title: Trie
item.description: Trie is a data structure that maintains a set S of strings in \Sigma^* by maintaining a deterministic finite automaton that accepts and only accepts strings in S.
